---
title: "PolyU HTML Demo"
subtitle: "Fixed Header and TOC Offset Check"
author: "Frank"
date: today
brand: _extensions/polyu/brand.yml
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
---

# Overview

This example page is for checking whether the `polyu-html` format applies the fixed top header correctly.

The left side of the header should show the PolyU logo image, and the right side should show the tagline image.

## What To Check

1. The header stays fixed at the top while scrolling.
2. The main content starts below the header and is not covered.
3. The table of contents is shifted down together with the page content.
4. Anchor jumps should stop below the header instead of hiding headings.

# Content Demo

This section adds enough structure to make the TOC visible and to test the spacing around headings, paragraphs, lists, and callouts.

## Typography

The page should still inherit the brand typography from the PolyU brand definition.

### Paragraph

PolyU HTML pages often need a compact but formal presentation style. This example keeps the layout simple so the header behavior is easy to inspect.

### List

- Brand colors should still apply.
- Link styling should remain readable.
- The header should not overlap the title block.

## Callout

::: {.callout-note}
## Note

If this note starts too close to the top of the page, the main content offset is not large enough.
:::

# Long Scroll Check

This section exists mainly to create vertical space for scrolling.

## Section A

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Integer posuere, libero a tristique viverra, justo dolor tristique arcu, sed luctus lorem libero non risus.

## Section B

Vivamus interdum, nunc ut posuere feugiat, sem nulla tincidunt nibh, vitae viverra nibh nisl at ipsum. Suspendisse potenti.

## Section C

Donec pretium, nibh in facilisis fermentum, massa sem posuere velit, at aliquam nunc eros non neque. [PolyU official website](https://www.polyu.edu.hk/).

# Final Check

Use this page to visually confirm the fixed header, content offset, and TOC offset together in one HTML document.
